rejectedContent.title: Sharing the Reward
rejectedContent.reason: Rejected because the reviewed source page appeared incomplete and was not safe to polish into the new book.

rejectedContent.title: Eating Sand
rejectedContent.reason: Replaced by “Face-Saving Formula,” which carries a cleaner sand-challenge thread inside the same trusted Tenali collection.

sourceNotes.title: Tenali stories
sourceNotes.note: The two retained stories are treated as concise folk retellings from the same Katha Kids collection, not as literal court history.

sourceNotes.title: Italian of the East
sourceNotes.note: Britannica anchors the language facts; Henry Morris is named directly while the phrase remains framed as a long-circulating nickname tied to sound rather than a hard classification.

sourceNotes.title: Vemana poem
sourceNotes.note: The poem page is grounded in the historical C.P. Brown edition while the public translation is newly rewritten for readability.

sourceNotes.title: Temples and Sriharikota
sourceNotes.note: Tirupati, Yadadri, and Sriharikota are internally grounded in official or institutional sources but presented publicly as elegant short reading pages.

editorialSoftening.action: Removed entirely from the public reading flow so the site behaves like a small book, not a reference ledger.
Kept as a graceful question-led explainer instead of a dramatic unsupported claim.
Left out in favor of widely documented engineering and public-service achievements.
Changed to one strong image per chapter so later story pages can breathe as text-only paper pages.

details: 15 total book screens resolved, including the cover.
2 story chapters present, each fixed at exactly 3 pages.
Public build keeps only / and /read/[slug] as book-facing routes.
Remote source and image URL checks were skipped for CI/deploy.
